rule: candidate outcome classes partition the generated obligation surface
applicable rows match the primary denominator
rejected rows remain visible outside the denominator
unsupported fragments remain visible outside the denominator
primary failures are computed only after applicability and support admission
representative rejection reasons are available for inspection
seeded positive-control rows match the frozen summary
seeded positive-control kills match the frozen summary
failure sensitivity is spread across more than one law family and drift operator
OPA/Rego hierarchy evidence is bounded to the normalized-decision fragment
outcome-independent holdout contains enough evaluated obligations for an audit surface
stress witnesses carry an explicit non-prevalence claim boundary
